sections.title: A: Word Formation
sections.instructions: Use the word given in capitals to form a word that fits in the space.

items.sentence: Despite being (1) ...... (DOUBT) at first, she eventually came to see the value of the (2) ...... (CONVINCE) evidence presented by the research team.
Her (3) ...... (EXPLAIN) of the concept was so clear and (4) ...... (IMAGINE) that even students with no prior knowledge were able to grasp it immediately.

The (5) ...... (ASSUME) that intelligence is fixed has been widely challenged, and scientists now argue that what determines potential is not genes but (6) ...... (DECIDE) action and consistent effort.
Her initial (7) ...... (CONFUSE) about the topic gradually gave way to a deep (8) ...... (BELIEVE) that she could master it with enough practice.
It is almost (9) ...... (CONCEIVE) that a person of such (10) ...... (BRILLIANT) could choose to work in complete obscurity for so long.

sections.title: B: Key Word Transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and eight words, including the word given.

items.sentence:
The university introduced its new curriculum in 2020 and still uses it today. **SINCE**
The university ………………………………………………………………… 2020.
items.answer: has been using its new curriculum since

items.sentence:
They finally managed to think of a plan to deal with the problem. **COME**
They have finally ………………………………………………………………… with the problem.
items.answer: come up with a plan to deal

items.sentence:
His constant habit of talking over people in meetings really frustrates his colleagues. **ALWAYS**
He ………………………………………………………………… in meetings, which really frustrates his colleagues.

The committee reached its conclusion only a few minutes ago. **JUST**
The committee ………………………………………………………………… conclusion.

The scientists haven't managed to figure out what caused the anomaly. **YET**
The scientists have ………………………………………………………………… what caused the anomaly.
items.answer: not yet managed to figure out

items.sentence:
It is now three hours since she started mulling over her decision. **BEEN**
She ………………………………………………………………… for three hours.
items.answer: has been mulling over her decision

items.sentence:
I have never come across such an ingenious solution before. **FIRST**
This is ………………………………………………………………… come across such an ingenious solution.

She's decided what to do about the offer and doesn't need more time. **MADE**
She ………………………………………………………………… the offer.
items.answer: has already made up her mind about

sections.title: C: Idioms / Collocations
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: In a fast-moving debate, you really need to have your ……………………………………… about you to respond quickly and effectively.
She felt completely in the ……………………………………… about the team's plans until she finally asked the manager directly.
When she spotted the familiar handwriting on the envelope, she immediately ……………………………………… two and two together.
He has always been quick on the ……………………………………… – he grasps new ideas before most people have even finished reading the instructions.
The name doesn't ……………………………………… a bell – are you sure we've met before?
The sheer volume of material she was expected to learn in a week was enough to drive anyone ……………………………………… the bend.
After the exam period, she decided to ……………………………………… stock of how well she had done and think about what she could improve.
Stop ……………………………………… hairs and give me a straight answer – do you think the plan is workable or not?
I haven't spoken French in years, so I really need to **brush / cram / pick** up on it before my trip.
It took her a few days to **come / turn / get** round to the idea of studying abroad, but eventually she was convinced.
The detectives spent hours trying to **piece / put / pull** together exactly what had happened on the night in question.
She **hit / came / fell** upon the perfect title for her essay just as she was about to give up and go to bed.
The new material was a lot to **take / make / get** in all at once, so the teacher suggested reviewing it in smaller sections.
He needed more time to **think / mull / turn** over the offer before giving a definitive answer.
She spent the afternoon trying to **figure / come / reach** out how the algorithm worked.
Before committing to a course of action, it is always worth taking the time to **think / talk / work** it through carefully.
The detective had a ...... that the key witness was not telling the whole truth.
Before making a final decision, the team took time to carefully ...... the risks involved.
The professor remained ...... about the study's methodology, arguing that the sample size was too small to draw definitive conclusions.
The engineer devised an ...... solution that nobody in the field had previously considered.
She sat quietly for a moment to ...... on what she had learned before writing up her notes.
He is one of the most ...... students I have ever taught – he always double-checks his work and never cuts corners.
She was faced with a ...... : she could accept the prestigious award or protect her privacy.
From the available evidence, the analysts were able to ...... that the decision had been made well in advance.
